name: Frijoles Puerto Nuevo
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp Shortening (lard or canola oil), divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the beans and soak them in water overnight.
2. In the same water, bring the beans to a boil and add onion. Let boil for about 1 hour, adding more water as it evaporates.
3. When the beans are almost done, add 4 tablespoons of shortening (lard or oil) and salt to taste. Let cool for 3 hours.
4. Bring back to a boil. Add remaining shortening and dried red chilies. When the beans begin to come apart and the broth has become muddy, they are done.
5. Shortening-less beans are called "Frijoles de la Olla" La Casa de la Langosta, Puerto Nuevo, Mexico (Baja). Riverside Press-Enterprise Interview 15 Aug 96. See "Lobster Puerto Nuevo" recipe for more information.
Recipe By: Joel Iraola, chef, La Casa de la Langosta
